notes: DDR5-6400 over the last 7-14 days stayed anchored by 64GB and 128GB, with 96GB only recently repaired and 32GB still weaker on exact public anchors.
DDR5-5600 remained structurally split at 32GB for the full review window; this remains outlier_only rather than no_public_sample.
PCIe4 TLC remained the most stable and executable category, with 30.72TB public coverage holding after the 2026-04-09 recovery.
PCIe5 TLC kept decent nominal coverage, but 30.72TB anchor decay stayed unresolved: the older lower-band reference has not returned cleanly since 2026-04-13.
PCIe4 QLC stayed fully covered, but part of the supplementation burden continues to shift to parser-gap and RFQ classification at Mouser, PCNation, Wiredzone, and anti-bot gated pages.

noDirectSampleSources: 16GB exact comparable part still not recovered

outlierOnlySources: A-Tech/Newegg/CDW Kingston low cluster vs Provantage/CDW/Connection high cluster for 32GB

noDirectSampleSources: Provantage/SHI lower-band exact 30.72TB public anchor still missing

keyChangesVsYesterday: No tracked representative value changed versus 2026-04-20; the market still looks like stable main bands plus unresolved structural splits.
DDR5-5600 32GB was rechecked with a directly fetchable CDW Kingston page at $895.99, strengthening the outlier_only classification rather than no_public_sample.
Mouser again returned direct denied content, which further confirms parser_gap for high-capacity PCIe4 QLC supplementation instead of true no-public-sample absence.
Newegg direct product access today mostly surfaced anti-bot / are-you-human pages, which is useful as access-structure evidence but not as a new clean representative quote.

DDR5-6400.reason: 64GB and 128GB remain stable, 96GB remains visible after the recent repair, but 32GB still lacks a stronger clean exact anchor and 16GB is still absent.

DDR5-5600.reason: 32GB remains structurally split across low retail and high distributor bands, while 64GB and 128GB remain visible but not at attractive low levels.

PCIe4 TLC.reason: This remains the most stable and fully covered category, with repeatable public distribution bands and no new sign of anchor decay.

PCIe5 TLC.reason: Lower capacities remain observable, but 30.72TB still lacks a trustworthy single representative public band because the old lower anchor remains stale while reproducible Micron/high-band quotes persist.

PCIe4 QLC.reason: Main premium public bands remain usable, but high-capacity low clusters and parser-gated supplementation remain too noisy to justify a one-shot bottom-call.

gaps.note: Today's multi-source checks still did not recover a clean enterprise single-stick public quote.
Today's multi-source checks still did not recover a clean enterprise single-stick public quote.
Low-side public pages remain far below the higher distributor band, and today's directly fetchable CDW Kingston price keeps the split alive rather than resolving it.
The previously preferred lower-band anchor still did not repeat cleanly; current evidence remains split between a sold-out low cluster and reproducible higher Micron bands.
The page again returned direct denied content, confirming access/parser failure rather than true absence of public market pricing.
